title: DCI Establishment of New Dental Colleges Regulations, 1993
description: DCI Establishment of New Dental Colleges Regulations, 1993
text: Mwmwwwwwe.m :‘,,.-..,..I,.«,;v ,_ “mm DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA NEW DELHI DENTAL COUNCIL or INDIA 5 Establishment of New Dental Colleges Regulations 1993 PUBLISHED AND SOLD BY : DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA AIWAN-E-GHALIB MARG, KOTLA ROAD NEW DELHI -- 1 of 12 -- DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA NEW DELHI Establishment of New Dental Coileges Regulations 1 993 Published and Sold by DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA . AIWAN—E—GHALIB MARG, KOTLA ROAD, NEW DELHI-l 10002if v , x . 1 -- 2 of 12 -- Regulations for: the Establishment of new Dental Colleges v In exercise of the powers conferred by section 10A read with section 20 of the Dentists Act. I948 (16 of I 948). the Dental Councilof India with the previous approval ofthe Central Govt. hereby makes the following Regulations: - 'Io (i) (ii) These regulations may becalled the Establishment 6? New Dental Colleges Regulations. 1993. They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the officiar gazette. Scheme relating to Dental Colleges The scheme referred to in clauses (a) and (b) of sub-section (2) of section 10 A of the Dentists Act. I948 (Modified upto 27.8. [992 is a part of the regulations and' Is annexed hereto.- I -- 3 of 12 -- SCHEME APPLICATION FOR PERMISSION OF THE CENTRAL GOVERNMENT TO ESTABLISH A NEW DENTAL COLLEGE Instructions to the Applicant Eligibility Criteria Qualifying Criteria 5‘wa The following organisations shall be eligible to apply for permission to set up new Dental Colleges : University and State Governments/Union Territories. Autonomous Bodies promoted by Central and State Governments. Societies registered under Societies Registration Act, 1860. Public Trusts, Religious or Charitable, registered under the lndian Trusts Act 1 882, Wakf Act etc. ' The applicants are requested to ensure that complete information, as detailed below, is furnished while submitting the application. ‘ ' The eligible organisations shall abide by the Dentists Act, 1948. and the Regu- lations framed thereunder and shall qualify to apply for permission to establish new Dental Colleges only if the following conditions are fulfilled: That Dental Education is one of the main objectives of the applicant. That a minimum of 5 acres of land’and with constructed area as shown below is owned and possessed by the applicant to set up the proposed Dental College Admissions 1st year 4th year 40 ’ 16,000 sq.ft 40,000 sq.ft 60 24,000,sq.rt 60,000 sq.ft 100 y - 40,000 sq.ft 100,000 sq.ft __________________.__—————————- - NB. 1 . Constructed area to be increased in a phased manner between 1 st and 4th year. I 2. ' Adequate Hostel accommodation and staff-accomodation should be available in addition to the above-mentioned built-up areaof the Dental College. ‘ That permission/essentiality certificate regarding the desirability and feasibility of having the proposed Dental College at the proposed location has been obtained bythe applicant from the respective State Government or the Union Territory Administration." ' that permission/letter of University's Affiliation for the proposed Dental College has been obtained by the applicant from a recognised University and that adequate clinical material is available for fulfilling the requirements of the syllabus and regulations laid down by tha.Dental Councilof India. .1 -- 4 of 12 -- 10. f 11.‘ 12. that the applicant has a feasible and time-bound programme to set up the proposed Dental College along with the required infrastructural facilities includ- ing adequate hostel facilities for boys and girls as prescribed by the Dental Council of indie, commensurate with the proposed intake of students, so as to complete the Dental College within a period of fouryears from the date of grant of permission. that the Dental College will admit students only after the Council has satisfied itself aboutthe infrastructural and otherfacilities forstarting B. D. S. Course and the College will admit students only after receiving a written permission from . the Central Government. that the applicant should be able to take admisSions to the College within two years of grant of permission and should complete total development of the Dental College within three years from the date of taking admissions. that the Dental College should be an independent institution with full comple- ment of staff. including the Principal/Dean. ‘ that the applicant shall locate the proposed college in proximity of a Medical College and shall getan undertaking ofthesaid Medical College to the effect that the said Medical College shall facilitate training to the students of the proposed Dental College inthe subjects of Medicine. Surgeryand Allied Medical Sciences. Where no Medical College is available in the proximity of the proposed Dental College. the proposed Dental College shall get itself tied up with a General Hospital which is having provision for atleast 100 beds and whichIs located within 10 K. M. radius of the proposed Dental College. it shall be the duty of the applicantto produce evidence thatthe infrastructural facIlmessuchasteaching, pro-clinical, para-clinicalandalliedrnedicalsciences are owned by the proposed Dental College itself. ' ' that the applicant hasia feasible and time-bound expansion programme to provide additionalequipmentsandinfrastructuralfaCIlItIes, as prescribed bythe Dental Council of India and as per detailed chart for Dental Chairs/Units, given below: ______—_——————— 40 ‘ so . 100 admissions admlsslons admlsslons tst year 10 10 10 2nd year 20 20 25 3rd year - 40 .‘ 60 1 00 4th year ' 80 120 200 5th year (Internship) 100 150 . 250 thatthe applicant has made provision forexpansion ofteaching staff in a phased manner as per Appendix: 1. 2 and 3 (Chart of Staff) that the applicant provides a performance bank-guaranteeIn favour of Dental Councilof lndiaforthe establishmentofthe Dental College and its infrastructural facilities as folldWs: -- 5 of 12 -- 10. 1'1“ 12. that the applicant has a feasible and time-bound programme to set up the proposed Dental College along with the required infrastructural facilities includ» ing adequate hostel facilities for boys and girls as prescribed by the Dental Council of India, commensurate with the proposed intake of students, so as to complete the Dental College within a period of fouryears from the date of grant of permission. that the Dental College will admit students only after the Council has satisfied itself aboutthe infrastructural and otherfacilities forstarting B.D.S. Course and the College will admit students only after receiving a written permission from , the Central GOvernment. ' that the applicant should be able to take admissions to the College within two years of grant of permissibn and should complete total development of the Dental College within three years from the date. of taking admissions. that the Dental College should be an independent institution with full comple- ment of staff, including the Principal/Deane ' that the applicant shall locate the proposed college in proximity of a Medical College and shall get an undertaking of the said Medical College to the effect that the said Medical College shall facilitate training to the students of the proposed Dental College in the subjects of'Medicine. Surgery andAllied Medical Sciences" Where no Medical College is available in the proximity of the proposed Dental College, the proposed Dental College shall get itself tied up with a General Hospital which is having provision for atleast 100 beds and which is located within 10 KM. radius of the proposed Dental College. 'lt Shall be the duty of the applicantto produce evidence thatthe infrastructural facilities suchas teaching, preclinical, para-clinical and allied medical sciences are owned by the'proposed Dental College itself. "- . ' that the applicant has-a feasible and time-bound expansion programme to provide additional equipments and infrastructuralfacilities, as prescribed bythe Dental Council of India and as perdetailed chartforDentai Chairs/Units, given below: '- ' 40 ‘ ‘ ‘50 . 100 . admissions admissions ’ admissions , 1st year ’ 10 to to 2nd year 20 20 g 25 3rd year " 4O .' ‘ 60 too 4th year 80 120 200 5th year (Internship) 100 _ ' 150 , 250 thatthe applicant has made proVisionforexpansion ofteaching staffinaphased manner as per Appendix: 1. aand 3 (Chart of Staff) that the applicant provides a performance bank—guarantee in tavour of Dental Council of lndiaforthe establishment ofthe DentalCollege and its inirastructu ral taciiities as tollOWs: ' y ” -- 6 of 12 -- . a”! Submisslonof Appllcatlonl Scheme and Appllcetlon Fee Functional programme: inclUding department-wise and service-Wise func- tional requirements and area distribution and room-wise seating capacity; Equipment programme; including room-wise list of dental, Scientific and allied equipment complete witlrschedule of quantities and specifications; Manpower programme: including department-wise requirements of teaching ‘ staff (full-time) technical, administrative and ancillary staff, category-wise 10. 11. recruitment criteria and salary structure etc. (minimum as per UGC scale); ‘ The details of arrangements for teaching of non-clinical, medical subjects' indication whether the arrangements are independent in the proposed Dental COIlege or facilities of a medical college will be utilised; Building programme: including building-wise built-up area of the Dental College, facultyand staff housing, staff and student hostels, administrative office, library, auditionum animal house and other infrastructural facilities such as cultural'and recreation centre Sports complex etc; Planning annd layout: including master plan of the Dental College complex, . layout plans, sections, elevations and floor-wise area calculations of the 12. 13. 14'. 15. 16. 17. Dental Collegean‘d ancillary building etc; Phasing andscheduling: including month-wiseschedule of activities, indicat- ingcommencement and completion of building design, local body approvals civil construction, provision cf engineering services and equipment, recruit- ment of staffand phased commissioning, to commensurate with the proposed intake of students, Project cost: including capital cost of land, buildings, plant and machinery, dental scientificand allied equipment, furniture and fixtures, and preliminary and pre-operative expenses; , Means of financing the project cost: including centribution of the applicant, grants and donations équity and term loans, and other sources if any; Revenue assumptions: including fee structure and estimated annual revenue from various soulrces; Expenditure assumptions: includingoperating expenses, financial expenses and depreciatibn; and ‘ Operating results: including income statements, cash flow statement and projected balance- sheets. The applicationshall be submitted by registered post only to the Secretary (Health),-Ministry of Health and'Famlly Welfare, GoVemment of lndia, Nirrnan . Bhavan, MaulanaAzad Road, New Delhi 110 011, alongwith a non-refundd able application feeof Rs 2.00 lakhs'In the form of a Demand Draft/Pay Order in favour of Dental Council of India and Payable at Delhi The application fee shall include registration fee, fee for technical scrutiny and fee for three inspections and other contingent expenditure. Beyondthree inspections the applicant has topay to the Council inspection fee prescribed by the Council from time to time. Incomplete appplicatidns-will not be accepted and will be 4 -- 7 of 12 -- \ Evaluation by the Dental Council of India Grant of. Permission returned by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare to the applicant, along with enclosures and application fee. Application found completeIn all respects will be registered by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare and forwarded tothe Dental Council of India within 30 days for evaluation and recommendations. Acceptance of the application will only signify the acceptance of application for evaluation. It will, however, under no circumstances, mean approval of the application for grant of permis- sion. . The period of one year prescribed under the Dentists(Amendment) Act, 1993 for processing of an application for setting up a new Dental College will commence from the date of registration of the application by the Ministry of * Health and Family Welfare. The Dental Council of India will evaluate the application to establish the desirability and prima-facie feasibilty of setting up the Dental College at the proposedlocation and the capability of the applicant to provide necessary resources and infrastructure for the scheme. While evaluating the application, the Council may seek further information/ clarification or additional documents from the applicant as considered neces- sary or may carry out physical inspection to vei‘ify the information- The central Government, on the recommendations of the Dental Council of India, may isSue a letter of intent to set up a new. Dental College with such conditions or modification in the original proposal as may be considered necessary. The formal permission will be granted after the above conditidns and modifications are accepted andtheperformance bank guarantees for the required sums are furnished by the applicant. The formal permission will include a time-bound programme for the establish- ment of the Dental College. This permission Will aISO include a clear-cut definition ofpreliminary requirements to be met in respect of buildings, infras- - tructural facilities, dental and allied equipment, faculty and staff etc. before admitting the first batch of Students. The permission will also define annual targets to be achieved by the applicant commensurate with the in-take of students during the following years The above permission to establish a new Dental. College and admit students will be granted for a period of one-year and will be renewed on yearly basis subject to verification of the achievement of annual targets and revalidation of the performance bank guarantees. This process of renewal of permission will continue till such time the establishment of the Dental College and expansion of the hospital facilitiesIs completed and a formal recognition will be granted after four years of the Dental College by. the Dental Council of India. Unless the college fulfills the requirements for various stages at development to the satisfaction of the Dental Council of India further admissions are liable to be stopped. - MB. The Council may obtain any other information from the proposed Dental College as it deems fit and necessary. 5 -- 8 of 12 -- ANNEXURE - “i ‘1' j‘PPLICATION FOR PERMISSION OF THE CENTRAL ~ _ ERNMENT To ESTABLISH A NEW DENTAL COLLEGE.
